meta.title: Free Percentage Calculator | Fast & Accurate Percent Math
meta.description: Easily calculate percentages online. Find percent of a number, convert to percent, and calculate increases or decreases.

seo.intro: Looking to calculate a percentage quickly and easily? On our site, our percentage calculator allows you to determine any percentage in seconds, effortlessly and without risk of error. Whether you want to calculate a percentage on a price, find a discount, or calculate an increase, our tool is made for you.

howTo.title: How to calculate a percentage easily
howTo.content: The basic formula for calculating a percentage is very simple:
howTo.example: Example: if you want to know what 45 out of 180 represents: (45 / 180) × 100 = 25%

situations.title: Percentage calculation: common situations
situations.content: Percentage calculations are used in many areas of daily life:
items: Commerce and sales: determining discounts, promotions and VAT
Personal finance: calculation of interest, savings and investments
Salaries: calculation of increases or decreases
Statistics and surveys: participation in events or election results

items.title: Example 1: Calculate a percentage on a price
items.content: Net price: $150 | Discount: 15% | Calculation: 150 × 0.15 = $22.50 | Final price: 150 – 22.50 = $127.50

items.title: Example 2: Calculate the percentage increase
items.content: Current salary: $2200 | Increase: 10% | Calculation: 2200 × 0.10 = $220 | New salary: 2200 + 220 = $2420

items.title: Example 3: Calculate percentage between two numbers
items.content: Total number: 500 | Number of participants: 325 | Calculation: (325 / 500) × 100 = 65 %

faq.question: How to calculate a percentage?
faq.answer: Divide the part by the total, then multiply by 100. Example: 50 out of 200 = (50/200) × 100 = 25%.

faq.question: How to do a percentage calculation quickly?
faq.answer: Use our free percentage calculator to get the result in seconds.

faq.question: What is the formula for calculating a percentage?
faq.answer: Percentage = (Partial value ÷ Total value) × 100. For increase or decrease, use ((New value – Old value) ÷ Old value) × 100.

faq.question: Can we calculate the percentage between two numbers?
faq.answer: Yes, divide the partial number by the total number and multiply by 100.

faq.question: How to calculate a percentage on a price?
faq.answer: Multiply the price by the percentage in decimal. Example: $200 × 0.15 = $30.

hero.title: Solve Any Percentage Problem Instantly

hero.trustText: Trusted by thousands of users worldwide

cardA.title: Percent to Number
cardA.formulaDescription: Our calculator uses the following formula: (p1 * v1) / 100 = result (where p1=percentage and v1=value). Example: (10 * 1000) / 100 = 100

cardB.title: Number to Percent
cardB.formulaDescription: Our calculator uses the following formula: (v1 / v2) * 100 = result (where v1=value and v2=total). Example: (50 / 1000) * 100 = 5%

cardC.title: Increase / Decrease
cardC.formulaDescription: Our calculator uses the following formula: v1 * (1 ± p1 / 100) = result (where v1=base and p1=percentage). Example: 1000 * (1 + 10 / 100) = 1100
cardC.labelBase: What is the percentage increase/decrease of

cardD.title: Percentage Change
cardD.formulaDescription: Our calculator uses the following formula: ((v2 - v1) / v1) * 100 = result (where v1=old and v2=new). Example: ((1200 - 1000) / 1000) * 100 = 20%
